segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: Listen to part of a lecture in an environmental science class.

segments.speaker: Professor
segments.text: Today we are going to talk about how wetlands help control flooding in nearby towns.

segments.speaker: Professor
segments.text: The key point is that the plants and soil can absorb water that would otherwise move very quickly downstream.

segments.speaker: Student
segments.text: So restoring a wetland can sometimes be cheaper than building a new concrete barrier.
